ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Jollibee-specific combo meal. FOODON_00002490 (fast food meal) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (composite fast food meal) is the closest available category. No Jollibee-specific or Filipino fast food combo code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific fast food combo meal product.

serving.description: 1 combo meal set (2 pc Chickenjoy + Spaghetti + 1 Peach Mango Pie + 1 Drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is the complete Fun for One Meal A combo, comprising two pieces of Chickenjoy fried chicken (approximately 280 g), one regular Jollibee Spaghetti (approximately 200 g), one Peach Mango Pie (approximately 100 g), and one regular soft drink (approximately 170 g / 170 ml), totalling approximately 750 g.

nutrient_source.title: Jollibee Philippines Nutritional Information — Chickenjoy (2 pc), Jolly Spaghetti, Peach Mango Pie, Regular Softdrink; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken, tomato meat pasta, fruit-filled pastry, and carbonated beverage
nutrient_source.organization: Jollibee Foods Corporation (official nutrition disclosure) / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Jollibee Philippines publishes per-item nutrition data. Component values were summed across the four meal items and converted to per-100 g basis using the estimated total serving weight of 750 g. USDA SR Legacy data for analogous items (fried chicken, pasta with meat sauce, fruit pie, cola) were used to cross-check and fill micronutrient gaps. Values represent a weighted composite estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; GI values for fried chicken (GI ~46), white pasta with meat sauce (GI ~45–55), fruit-filled pastry (GI ~59), and cola/carbonated soft drink (GI ~63) used as component analogues; weighted composite GI estimated at 62
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Jollibee combo meal. A weighted composite GI of 62 was estimated using the carbohydrate-weighted average of GI values for the four meal components: fried chicken (~46, low carb contribution), spaghetti with meat sauce (~52, medium GI), peach mango pie pastry (~59, medium GI), and regular cola (~63, medium GI). Carbohydrate contributions per component were used as weights. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (62 × 183.75) / 100 ≈ 113.9, rounded to 113.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 24.5 g/100 g × 750 g = 183.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 3150 mg sodium per full combo serving (420 mg per 100 g × 750 g), substantially exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this meal, as a single serving provides approximately 158% of the daily sodium limit; if consumed, pair with low-sodium foods for the remainder of the day and avoid adding condiments.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load per serving (GL ≈ 113) driven by refined carbohydrates from spaghetti, pastry, and sugar-sweetened beverage, with a composite GI of 62 (medium) but very large carbohydrate quantity
type2_diabetes.guidance: This meal is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its very high glycemic load; if consumed occasionally, the soft drink should be replaced with water or a sugar-free option, and portion size should be halved to reduce postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high sodium (≈3150 mg/serving), saturated fat (≈16.5 g/serving), trans fat (≈0.75 g/serving), and cholesterol (≈315 mg/serving) presents multiple cardiovascular risk factors in a single meal
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal due to its high saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and cholesterol content; if consumed very occasionally, the fried chicken skin should be removed, the soft drink replaced with water, and the pie omitted to reduce the lipid and glycemic burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Jollibee Fun for One Meal A with Drink (also listed as Fun for One Meal A (D)) is a complete fast food combo meal sold by Jollibee, the Filipino quick-service restaurant chain, at its Singapore outlet in Bugis Village. The meal consists of four components: two pieces of Chickenjoy — Jollibee's signature bone-in, skin-on deep-fried chicken coated in a crispy seasoned breading; one regular serving of Jollibee Spaghetti — a Filipino-style sweet tomato-based pasta topped with ground meat, sliced hotdog, and a sprinkle of cheese; one Peach Mango Pie — a deep-fried or baked golden pastry filled with sweet peach and mango filling; and one regular soft drink (typically cola or juice). The meal is halal-certified at the Singapore outlet. Nutritionally, the full combo is calorie-dense (approximately 1485 kcal per serving), high in refined carbohydrates, sodium, and saturated fat, making it a high-concern meal for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The composite glycemic index is estimated at 62 (medium GI), but the glycemic load per serving is very high at approximately 113 due to the large carbohydrate quantity from pasta, pastry, and sugar-sweetened beverage. The meal is culturally significant as a representation of Filipino fast food culture adapted for the Southeast Asian market, and is popular as an affordable all-in-one lunch or dinner option in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated from Jollibee Philippines official nutrition disclosures (per-item data) summed across four meal components and converted to per-100 g basis, cross-checked against USDA SR Legacy analogues. No Singapore-specific or HPB-sourced nutrient data exists for this exact combo meal. GI is a weighted composite estimate from approved GI database analogues. Confidence is medium due to the adapted/estimated nature of both nutrient and GI data.
notes: 1. Serving weight of 750 g is an estimate: 2 pc Chickenjoy ≈ 280 g, Spaghetti ≈ 200 g, Peach Mango Pie ≈ 100 g, Regular drink ≈ 170 g. 2. Per-100 g values are derived by dividing summed component nutrient totals by 750 g. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure or USDA SR Legacy analogues for this meal type. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure or USDA SR Legacy analogues for this meal type. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the Jollibee nutrition disclosure or USDA SR Legacy analogues for this meal type. 6. GI of 62 is a carbohydrate-weighted composite estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific product. 7. The meal is tagged Halal per Jollibee Singapore's halal certification. 8. This record is based on the Bugis Village outlet listing on field survey data; menu composition may vary by outlet or promotional period. 9. The soft drink component is assumed to be a regular (sugar-sweetened) cola or juice; if a diet/zero-sugar drink is substituted, sugar_g and energy_kcal per serving would be lower.
